title: Fix a compile-time Application.get_env with runtime enqueue-time overrides
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/compile_time_queue_worker.ex` calls `Application.get_env/2` inside `use Oban.Worker` — `use` is a compile-time macro, so the values freeze at compile time. Test configuration and prod configuration collide at boot. Rewrite the module to: (1) `use Oban.Worker` with STATIC queue `:exports` and STATIC `max_attempts: 3`, (2) add a public `enqueue/1` function that accepts an `export_id` and reads `Application.get_env(:my_app, :export_queue, :exports)` and `Application.get_env(:my_app, :export_attempts, 3)` at RUNTIME, then calls `new(%{"export_id" => export_id}, queue: queue, max_attempts: max_attempts)` and `Oban.insert/1`. Add a `@moduledoc` explicitly citing the Oban.Worker docs warning: 'Like all use macros, options are defined at compile time. Avoid using Application.get_env/2 to define worker options.' Include a mention of how test/prod config leak happens. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/export_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding Elixir's compile-time macro semantics, the Oban.Worker docs warning, and designing a runtime escape hatch. Production-grade knowledge that rarely shows up in LLM output without priming.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'Like all use macros, options are defined at compile time. Avoid using Application.get_env/2 to define worker options.'
